notes: status.json is authoritative for hub state.
.plans carries planning, research, handoffs, readiness, and evolving implementation context.
Runtime contracts that code depends on belong under packages.
This hub owns the public website design package, research inputs, and implementation sequence.

2026-05-24 modern Web UI follow-up: added reports/modern-web-ui-follow-up-2026-05-24.md and checklist items for text-scale readiness, Astro MPA View Transitions, CSS scroll spy, scroll-state header behavior, closedby dialog polish, and stale parallax/script review. Lane statuses are unchanged.

2026-05-24 Chrome platform operationalization: added next proof tasks for isolated Chrome DevTools MCP evidence, CWV/navigation evidence fields, and a frozen runtime WebMCP approval spec. Lane statuses are unchanged.

history.note: Scaffolded feature hub in active
Folded temporary docs, research, prompts, and high-fidelity design artifacts into this active hub so root docs do not become stale parallel truth.
Recorded the data-first foundation wave before visual implementation: Keystatic shape, private intake APIs, normalized migrations, and Green Goods impact cache wiring.

Completed the public Schema Delta Pass in 996ce51: Astro/Keystatic schemas now cover HiFi home, garden, library, stories, chapters, guilds, resources, and book compatibility surfaces; Keystatic access control is documented as local file-backed authoring only.

Completed the foundation pass before page implementation: ported HiFi gp-tokens.css into packages/website with only the Astro public asset URL adapter, added a noindex /design-system token and component review surface, and introduced the new GpLayout, public shell components, and reusable Astro primitives under packages/website. GpLayout preserves the existing stylized background image by default while keeping the HiFi topographic texture as an opt-in section utility.
Addressed foundation review findings: made link-like primitives render non-clickable markup when no URL is supplied and added the skip-to-content shell accessibility path.

Restored Garden shell navigation and added a minimal public /garden route backed by the existing garden singleton with public-safe fallbacks, leaving the full HiFi Garden page for its focused implementation pass.

Completed Step 3.5 content grounding before Home implementation: added a source-backed content seed inventory, seeded public Keystatic content across the target collections, added an optional idempotent public runtime seed fixture/script for approved map nodes and one normalized chapter impact snapshot, and validated build plus plan and runtime contract checks.

Addressed the production review findings for Step 3.5: review-gated chapters are excluded from generated chapter pages, the chapter index, locations.json, and impact source exports; seeded stories now have draft status and are filtered from public story routes until steward approval; SEO canonical/noindex metadata is wired through active layouts; and unhealthy Germany/Uncommons proof links were removed from launch content.

Implemented the map polish and Live Onboarding Mode slice: admin-owned intake settings, live auto-approval with private audit rows, public-safe intakeMode map state, theme edge rendering, distinct chapter/steward/member node colors, and a responsive add-node flow.

Addressed the second production review findings: moved Live Onboarding settings into a replay-safe 003 migration, removed public refs to draft stories and noindex chapters, added a public content contract test for generated chapter/published story refs, and refreshed the Step 3.5 readiness notes.

Captured the Email Magic Link Node Updates pass as plan-only work: email-only ownership for submitted map nodes, hashed one-use edit tokens, pending update requests, neutral edit-link responses, steward approval before public changes, and no wallet/local-secret ownership in v1.

Resolved remaining map-plan gaps for the Email Magic Link Node Updates pass: required email on new submissions, exact public route shapes, 15-minute edit-link cooldown, token validation versus consumption semantics, editable public field list, and neutral responses for no-match and cooldown cases.

Added final hardening notes for the Email Magic Link Node Updates plan: neutral email-provider failure behavior, raw-token hygiene before analytics/navigation, and Directus permission boundaries for token/email/request metadata.

Added final implementation-readiness gaps to the map and email-update plan: migration ordering, role/type edit boundaries, stale approval protection, token/request retention, live map cache behavior, broader edit-link abuse limits, Directus review ergonomics, and accessible map decluttering.

Implemented the public website HiFi surfaces against real seeded content: new GpLayout-backed Home with an Astro-native mycelial map driven by real chapter lat/long and shared-theme threads, podcast + active-chapter proof stats only, library bento, ecosystem grid, and Garden ramp; rebuilt Chapters index + chapter detail with featured tier, region/status filter, proof links, impact cache, related chapters; rebuilt Library, Stories index (empty state for draft-only seeded stories), Story detail, initial guild index/detail work, and Garden; polished Garden hero with hero pill imagery; moved retired entry CTAs to Garden and real public URLs; added prerender directive to locations.json and impact-sources.json. bun run build, test:content, test:agent, test:map-nodes, and plans:validate all pass.

Production-quality + responsive deep review. Punch-listed and fixed: home library bento was using broken nth-of-type selectors against mixed sibling types (dev/writers guild tiles fell to auto-flow); now uses unique modifier classes. Breadcrumb double-padded inside Container — removed horizontal padding. Added ogType prop to GpLayout, set 'profile' on chapter/guild detail and 'article' on story detail. Chapter steward chips now render display names with role rather than lowercased slugs. LinkRow auto-derives smart glyphs from labels (KG, GH, GT, PG, RH, X) instead of taking the first two letters. Home book grid no longer opens internal fallback in new tab. Filter pills bump to 44px on touch devices via (hover: none, pointer: coarse). Home hero proof stats tightened on mobile. Stories empty state copy is now count-driven and ages gracefully. Footer wordmark alt updated to 'Greenpill Network'. Featured chapter grid is now count-tolerant via count-N modifier class. Sticky filter top now references new --gp-header-height token. HomeMap nodes/halos enlarged at ≤720px via CSS-overridden SVG r= attribute; legend trims to top 5 items at ≤480px. Verified 167+ @media queries across pages, 14 chapter pins + 68 mycelial threads rendered, 35-page build still clean, every test suite passes.

Implemented the Email Magic Link Node Updates platform/API foundation: required valid owner email on new public map-node submissions, added replay-safe 007 edit-token/update-request migration with hashed one-use 30-minute tokens, one-pending-request stale-write protection, approval trigger, and cleanup path; added neutral edit-link, edit-session, and update-request route contracts with Resend fetch sending, cooldown/daily abuse buckets, generic invalid-link errors, token consumption after successful pending-request insert, Directus review-safe permissions, env docs, and contract coverage. Website /map/edit UX, selected-node update entry point, browser token URL cleanup, and mobile visual QA remain explicit follow-up work.

Completed the typed-foundation pass for the non-website runtime: packages/shared and packages/agent now compile from TypeScript source into dist exports, repo runtime/contract scripts run as Bun TypeScript, root typecheck builds the package graph, package imports keep the existing @greenpill-network/* specifiers, and validation passes for typecheck, map-node, agent, content, chapter-impact, plan validation, and website build. Remaining JavaScript/MJS files are website/config/browser surfaces outside this runtime pass.

Addressed typed-foundation review findings: the agent Docker build stage now installs TypeScript build tooling before compiling package dist, package-local website and agent commands build shared/agent dist before runtime execution, and Node type declarations are pinned to the Node 22 runtime major. Root validation, package contract tests, website build, Docker build-stage, and Docker production-deps stage pass; full final Docker image build was blocked only by repeated Docker Hub node:22-alpine TLS timeouts.

Corrected homepage and visible navigation truth: removed over-broad homepage proof stats for continent and steward counts, kept homepage-safe proof to podcast and chapter counts, completed visible retired-entry cleanup by routing legacy Nav, Garden fallbacks, and onboarding CTAs to Garden destinations while preserving the temporary legacy redirect.
Fully retired the former join route after product signoff: removed the remaining Astro redirect, leaving Garden as the visible entry point and no active legacy join route in the website source.

Locked the current public website route contract in the active plan: Garden owns onboarding, guild navigation links directly to Dev Guild and Writers Guild, project records remain internal proof/reference data only, and the retired onboarding, root guild directory, and project route families must not be reintroduced.

Addressed validated map follow-ups within Codex scope: stale interactive map intake now requires a private owner email before saving local pending state, the edit-flow retention path has an operator cleanup command, contract tests cover both safeguards, and migration replay plus cleanup execution were proven against local Postgres with an explicit local DATABASE_URL. The full selected-node edit-link website UX remains the planned follow-up lane.

Completed the current deep production review for the public website and map edit-flow changes: fixed valid-email gating before local pending map-node storage, fixed the optional map-edit browser smoke command, verified route retirement and stat-scope guardrails, and passed typecheck, build, map-node, agent, content, chapter-impact, map-edit route, optional browser-smoke skip, plan tests, and plan validation. Remaining work is the selected-node Update this node entry point, neutral edit-link request UI, and mobile visual QA.

Normalized active page typography to remove viewport-scaled font sizes and negative tracking, moved page hero scaling onto fixed breakpoint tokens, and added a public-content contract test so active Astro pages cannot reintroduce those typography regressions quietly.

Completed a focused Library UI and shared-shell background pass: kept the map lane untouched, made the sticky header and footer translucent over the site-wide graphic background, rebuilt the Library around the HiFi sequence with an editorial hero, cardless book shelf, podcast feature plus public episode rows, guild cards, and Garden/resource cards, and added shell-background contract coverage.

Implemented the selected-node 'Update this node' UI pass on the single home map and retired the competing ChapterMap.astro/scripts/map.ts surface. HomeMap now keeps its SSR mycelial map as a graceful no-JS baseline and progressively enhances: fetches /map/state with cache no-store, injects only approved-submission nodes as distinct teal member nodes, opens an in-flow selected panel on member activation, and offers owner edit-link requests for approved nodes only with neutral copy ('If this email matches the node owner...') shown on every post-validation outcome including network failure. Tokens stay on /map/edit only; no email/token in datasets, URLs, or storage. Added a mobile-safe collapsed add-node panel that writes local pending state only after the server accepts (approved skips local; reject/failure writes nothing). Browser QA found and fixed two real runtime bugs (panel/form queries were scoped to the canvas not the figure; JS-created nodes/chips lacked Astro's style-scope attribute) plus friendlier add-node network-error copy. Updated the map-node contract test to target HomeMap and assert the strengthened ordering, neutral edit-link, and token-surface boundary. Validation green: typecheck, test:agent, test:map-nodes, build:website, plans:validate.

Demo mobile-polish pass (Home, Library, Garden primary; Chapters, Stories secondary QA); map lane untouched. Home: fixed a 375px Stories-bento break that the verifier missed — the still-active max-width:1100px tablet placements (.count-4 .is-row-N, specificity 0,4,0) outranked the 720px mobile reset (.gp-home-story, 0,2,0), so cards fell into implicit grid columns with clipped text; the mobile reset now matches 0,4,0. Converted both viewport @media reflow blocks to @container against #main-content per DESIGN.md (tablet/desktop bento confirmed intact at 1024/1440); min-height 100vh -> 100dvh; garden-ramp step CTAs are now ghost so the hero keeps the single lime primary. Garden: added real #assessment and #steward-call target panels (public-safe assessmentPreview/stewardCallPreview kicker+title with honest framing and scroll-margin for the sticky header) so the Step 3/4 CTAs and Home's garden ramp now land; removed the DESIGN.md-forbidden infinite float/pulse on the hero pill, keeping a static glow. Library: added the book-rail edge-fade mask affordance and logical inset props on the resource arrow. Chapters: bumped filter pills (36px) and featured-card link rows (38px) to 44px at mobile width (the coarse-pointer rule the verifier cannot emulate), and promoted the Featured/Full-directory section labels to h2 to fix the h1->h3 heading-order jump (new optional, backward-compatible Overline as= prop; scoped styles keep the overline look). Verification: bun run ui:verify / /library /garden /chapters /stories = 0 HARD, 0 WARN at 375/1024/1440 (was 0 HARD / 29 WARN, all on /chapters); test:content 12 pass; plans:validate 7 hubs; website build clean. Shared touch: components/ui/Overline.astro (optional as prop, default div). Mobile Container gutter held at 24px (shared primitive; screenshots showed no cramping and Chapters/Stories collapse tight at <=520px). Console/runtime not separately audited beyond the headless render; the only client JS on these surfaces is HomeMap (map lane) and the unchanged Chapters filter script.

Opened the P0 public map recovery after read-only audit and product decisions. This supersedes prior map-complete and visual-QA-complete claims for the map slice: stewards must be public opt-in/allowlisted nodes, live sessions auto-approve valid submissions in real time, the then-proposed anonymous-density recovery path required explicit product confirmation and was later superseded by the unified real-node-only recovery, the theme picker is up to four themes, June 10 is a member self-add activity rather than a public-note feature, and Linear/production mutations remain separate approval-only work. Recovery report: reports/public-map-recovery-2026-05-21.md.

Applied the unified Home map recovery locally: the active hub remains the single plan source, generated anonymous density nodes are removed, public map state is chapters plus approved real member/steward submissions, steward allowlist entries can map email to trusted chapterSlug, public nodes reserve optional bioregion for future approved values without claiming polygon lookup, edges are trusted steward-to-chapter plus shared-theme relationships with per-node caps, and HomeMap selected-node details remain public-safe. Focused shared and agent contract tests pass; browser proof remains an explicit follow-up.

Addressed Home map recovery review findings locally: bioregion is an honest optional field with no fake pending fallback or relationship scoring, the Home map uses a count-free node identity legend instead of 0 steward/member counts, and scripts/home-map-browser-smoke.ts now exercises the real homepage add-node flow with mocked moderated/live agent responses, allowlisted steward rendering, selected-node relationship summary, and private-field DOM/storage guards. Validation passed for map-node contracts, agent contracts, Home browser smoke, website build, ui:check, ui:verify /, and plan validation.
Recorded the cross-repo modern CSS/Web UI audit follow-up inside this existing active hub without changing the current P0 map/design lane statuses or authorizing runtime CSS edits.

Operationalized Chrome platform follow-up tasks in plan.todo.md: isolated DevTools MCP proof for the Astro View Transition opt-in, CWV/navigation evidence fields, and frozen WebMCP approval-spec requirements. No lane status changed.
